## Objective
The user asked Goose to build a Tamagotchi-style virtual pet game with a pixelated/retro aesthetic that would run as a desktop application. The game needed to include stat management (hunger, happiness, health, energy), interactive care buttons, animated sprites, and a time-based gameplay loop where the pet's needs decay over time.

## Tools & Actions Used
- **todo__todo_write**: Created and maintained a development checklist to track progress across all game features (pixelated styling, stat system, game mechanics, sprite animations, time-based loop)
- **apps__create_app**: Generated a complete, self-contained HTML/CSS/JavaScript Tamagotchi app called `pixel-pet-tamagotchi` with the following capabilities:
  - Pixel art pet sprite with multiple state-based animations (happy, sad, sleeping, sick)
  - Four stat bars (Hunger, Happiness, Health, Energy) with real-time decay
  - Five interactive buttons: Feed, Play, Sleep, Medicine, Rename
  - Digital clock display and retro Game Boy-inspired color palette
  - Automatic stat decay loop running every few seconds
- **todo__todo_write** *(second call)*: Updated the checklist to mark all items as complete after the app was deployed

## Results & Outcomes
The Pixel Pet Tamagotchi was successfully created and automatically launched in a sandboxed app window. All requested features were implemented:
- Fully functional game loop with time-based stat decay
- Retro pixel art styling using the "Press Start 2P" font and a Game Boy-inspired green/gray palette
- Interactive pet that reacts visually to user actions and changes expression based on its current condition
- Pet can get sick if neglected, adding real consequence to the gameplay

The app is persistent and accessible from the Goose Apps tab.
